rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM H BRADLEY VS. DOLMEN PROPERTY GROUP LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday October 11, 2018 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT STEWART TITLE OF CALIFORNIA, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT continued to November 9, 2018. By no later than October 23, 2018 cross-complaint shall file a supplemental opposition limited to addressing SOL arguments on the merits. By no later than November 1, 2018 Cross-Defendant shall file a supplemental reply, if any, limited to SOL argument. While CCP 430.41 does not authorize bringing arguments on a subsequent demurrer that could have been brought in the prior demurrer(s), it would be a significant waste of resources under the circumstances of this case to not address this argument at this stage. Assuming the demurrer is overruled on other grounds (the Court does not make such determination at this time), cross-defendant will raise the SOL issue on Judgment on the Pleadings or Summary Judgment. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: AZIZEH BAJJALIYA ET AL VS. DAVID ABRAMOVITZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday October 11, 2018 line 6. DEFENDANT DAVID ABRAMOVITZ DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Defendant demurs to the entire complaint (See Notice of Demurrer). Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a demurrer may be sustained to a cause of action not asserted against this defendant. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KATIA BONILLA VS. SHELLPOINT MORTGAGE SERVICING ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday October 11, 2018 line 9. DEFENDANT NEW PENN FINANCIAL LLC D/B/A SHELLPOINT MORTGAGE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Violation of debt collection laws is sufficiently alleged (See paragraphs 35 and 36). These activities are outside of the foreclosure proceedings and Plaintiffs allegations must be taken as true at this stage of the litigation. Defendant admits that UCL cause of action survives with the first cause of action being a predicate for it. Plaintiff sufficiently alleged loss of money due to unfair/unlawful practices. Plaintiff sufficiently alleged violation of CC 2923.5 and the veracity of these allegations is not properly challenged. There is an actual controversy regarding the amounts owed under the Note/DOT and parties obligations on the contract going forward; and no other causes of action address this issue. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts giving rise to the duty. Therefore, demurrer is overruled as each cause of action is sufficiently alleged. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN YUDOWITZ VS. MART BAILEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday October 11, 2018 line 8. DEFENDANT MART BAILEY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. This unlawful detainer is based, inter alia, on the following allegations: "you removed the kitchen and added a bedroom in its place without permits. You damaged the toilet and bathtub and both are malfunctioning." This is sufficient to state a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that such actions by Defendants (assuming allegations are true for the purposes of demurrer) do not constitute illegal purpose and/or nuisance. = (501/CFH)
